title: Anthoshorea farinosa
intro: Anthoshorea farinosa, commonly known as Marsh Cinquefoil or Sweet Cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, diarrhea, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Anthoshorea farinosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Marsh Cinquefoil is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and as a natural anti-inflammatory remedy.
